zh.Sevilla, Seville, Andalucia, Spain: 西班牙安達盧希亞澤維利亞澤維利亞
zh.lifeSummary: 當Pedro Lopez Padilla Ponce De Leon Ballestero To King Pedro I於1260年出生在西班牙時，他的父親Lope Garcia Padilla30歲，母親Mrs. Lope Garcia Padilla26歲。他和Maria Gonzalez de Leiva至少有1個兒子。他去世在西班牙安達盧希亞澤維利亞澤維利亞。

zh-hans.Sevilla, Seville, Andalucia, Spain: 西班牙安達盧希亞澤維利亞澤維利亞
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Pedro Lopez Padilla Ponce De Leon Ballestero To King Pedro I于1260年出生在西班牙时，他的父亲Lope Garcia Padilla30岁，母亲Mrs. Lope Garcia Padilla26岁。他和Maria Gonzalez de Leiva至少有1个儿子。他去世在西班牙安達盧希亞澤維利亞澤維利亞。

nameMeanings.name: Padilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Luis, Carlos, Jesus, Ramon, Pedro, Miguel, Ruben, Francisco, Jorge.

Spanish: habitational name from any of various minor places, for example in the provinces of Burgos, Guadalajara, and Valladolid, named from Spanish padilla ‘frying pan, breadpan’ (from Latin patella, a diminutive of patina ‘shallow dish’), a word that was commonly used in the topographic sense of a shallow, gentle depression. In the US, this surname is also found among Native Americans (mainly Pueblos).

variants.name: Padillo
meanings: Spanish: altered form of Padilla , with the male suffix -o for matching with the sex of the bearer of the surname.

nameMeanings.name: Ponce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Luis, Jesus, Carlos, Francisco, Jorge, Miguel, Pedro, Raul, Roberto.

Spanish and French: from the medieval personal name Ponce, ultimately from Pontius, a Roman surname, perhaps an ethnic name for someone from Pontus (named from Greek pontos ‘ocean’) in Asia Minor, or an Italic cognate of Latin Quintus ‘fifth’ (i.e. ‘fifth-born’). The name was borne by two 3rd-century Christian saints, a Carthaginian deacon and a martyr of Nice, but was not widely popular in the Middle Ages because of the inhibiting influence of the even more famous Pontius Pilate. In some cases though, the surname may have been originally used for someone who had played the part of this character in a religious play.

variants.name: Ponce de Leon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Cesar, Mercedes, Alberto, Francisco, Javier, Juan, Lazaro, Leticia, Manuel, Ramon.
Spanish (Ponce de León): compound of the surname Ponce + the habitational name León (see Leon ).

History: Juan Ponce De León, the Spanish explorer who discovered Florida, came from a noble Aragonese family. He is believed to have been on board for Columbus's second voyage to Hispaniola in 1493. After his death in Florida from a wound from an Indian arrow in 1521, Ponce De León's title and rights in Florida were passed on to his son.

variants.name: Points
meanings: English (of Norman origin):
from the Old French personal name Ponz, Ponce, from Latin Pontius (see Ponce ).

habitational name from any of several places called Ponts (such as those in Manche and in Seine-Maritime, Normandy), from Latin pontes ‘bridges’. This name has been established since the 13th century in Ireland, where it appears as Pounce and Pounche, and is Gaelicized as Púinse.

variants.name: Pons
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Pedro, Ramon, Guillermo, Roberto, Carlos, Francisco, Luis, Mario, Rafael, Apolonia.  French Adrien, Armand, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Marcelle, Matilde.
Catalan, French (Occitan), and Dutch: from the medieval personal name Pons (see Ponce ).
German: patronymic from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name, such as Bonhard, formed with the element bon ‘request, petition’ (compare Latin bonus).

variants.name: Ponzio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Carlo, Carmine, Leonardo, Pasquale, Rocco, Salvatore, Silvio, Vincenzo.
Italian: from the personal name Ponzio, from Latin Pontius (see Ponce ).

variants.name: Poncelet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Benoit.  German Erwin, Fritz.
French (mainly northeastern) and Walloon:
topographic name from a diminutive of Old French poncel ‘little bridge’, or a habitational name from (Le) Poncelet, the name of several places in the northeastern part of France.

variants.name: Poinsett
meanings: Altered form of French Poinset: from a pet form of the personal name Poinse, from Latin Pontius (see Ponce ). Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t. The surname Poinset is very rare in France.

History: The poinsettia takes its name from an American diplomat, Joel Poinsett (1779–1851), who was a keen amateur botanist and who brought the flower back from Mexico, where he was US ambassador. He was born in Charleston, SC, and was descended from a Huguenot, Pierre Poinset, who was born in Soubise in Charente-Maritime and settled in Charleston c. 1700. Pierre Poinset is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar registers of the Huguenot Society of America and of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Point
meanings: French: from the personal name Point, from Latin Pontius (see Ponce ).
English: variant of Points with loss of final -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Leon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Pedro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Lopez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Late Latin name Lupus ‘wolf’, probably adopted as a calque of the Germanic name Wolf .

en.lifeSummary: When Pedro Lopez Padilla Ponce De Leon Ballestero To King Pedro I was born in 1260, in Spain, his father, Lope Garcia Padilla, was 30 and his mother, Mrs. Lope Garcia Padilla, was 26. He had at least 1 son with Maria Gonzalez de Leiva. He died in Sevilla, Sevilla, Andalusia, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Pedro Lopez Padilla Ponce De Leon Ballestero To King Pedro I est né en 1260, à Espagne, son père, Lope Garcia Padilla, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Lope Garcia Padilla, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Maria Gonzalez de Leiva. Il est décédé à Sevilla, Sevilla, Andalucía, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Pedro Lopez Padilla Ponce De Leon Ballestero To King Pedro I 1260 in Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lope Garcia Padilla 30 und seine Mutter Mrs. Lope Garcia Padilla 26. Er hatte mit Maria Gonzalez de Leiva mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in Sevilla, Sevilla, Andalucía, Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Pedro Lopez Padilla Ponce De Leon Ballestero To King Pedro I è nato il nel 1260 nel Spagna, il suo padre, Lope Garcia Padilla, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Lope Garcia Padilla, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Maria Gonzalez de Leiva. Morì nel Sevilla, Sevilla, Andalucía, Spagna.

ja.Sevilla, Seville, Andalucia, Spain: スペインアンダルクiアセヴィラセヴィラ
ja.lifeSummary: Pedro Lopez Padilla Ponce De Leon Ballestero To King Pedro Iが1260年にスペインで生まれた時，父Lope Garcia Padillaは30歳，母Mrs. Lope Garcia Padillaは26歳であった。彼はMaria Gonzalez de Leivaとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼はスペインアンダルクiアセヴィラセヴィラで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Pedro Lopez Padilla Ponce De Leon Ballestero To King Pedro I(이)가 1260년 스페인에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lope Garcia Padilla(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Lope Garcia Padilla(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Maria Gonzalez de Leiva(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 스페인 앤다루ci아 세비라 세비라에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Pedro Lopez Padilla Ponce De Leon Ballestero To King Pedro I nasceu em 1260, em Espanha, seu pai, Lope Garcia Padilla, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Lope Garcia Padilla, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Maria Gonzalez de Leiva. Ele faleceu em Sevilla, Sevilla, Andaluzia, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Pedro Lopez Padilla Ponce De Leon Ballestero To King Pedro I родился в 1260 г. в местности Испания, его отцу по имени Lope Garcia Padilla было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Lope Garcia Padilla, 26. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Maria Gonzalez de Leiva. Он умер в местности севилла, севилла, андалукiа, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Pedro Lopez Padilla Ponce De Leon Ballestero To King Pedro I nació en 1260, en España, su papá, Lope Garcia Padilla, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Mrs. Lope Garcia Padilla, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Maria Gonzalez de Leiva. Murió en Sevilla, Sevilla, Andalucía, España.
